insight: Qatar's performance in the 2026 World Cup has shown a notable impact from hydration breaks, with the team gaining momentum in both of their breaks so far. This momentum gain is reflected in a mean increase of 0.150 points per minute, indicating a slight but significant improvement in their performance after the breaks. Notably, when Qatar wasn't pressing before a hydration break, they still managed to maintain a positive momentum gain, suggesting that the breaks themselves may be providing a boost to their gameplay. Overall, the statistics suggest that hydration breaks have been beneficial for Qatar, allowing them to regain energy and come back stronger in the match.
